name: openclaw-backup
tagline: ClawHub (87★): Backup and restore OpenClaw data. Use when user asks to create backups, set up automatic backup schedules, restore from back
verdict: The cron job invokes ~/.openclaw/backup.sh while the docs say run ./scripts/backup.sh - unreconciled paths, so the automated backup can silently point at the wrong or nonexistent script. Restore is just as fragile: no failure guidance, and a repeated restore nests directories via mv instead of failing safely.

highlights.summary: The cron payload invokes ~/.openclaw/backup.sh, but the 'Create Backup' section (line 15) instructs running ./scripts/backup.sh — two different, unreconciled paths for what appears
No failure-path guidance anywhere in the document: gateway stop/start, mv, and tar extraction in the restore flow have no mention of what to do if any step fails (e.g. archive miss
Re-running the quick-restore steps a second time has no existence check: if ~/.openclaw-old already exists from a prior restore, mv will nest the directory inside it instead of the
